pattern: When a per-node choice can compound down a rooted tree (here, repeatedly halving a value), the useful state is capped because a bounded value can only be halved a handful of times before it floors at zero -- so a small per-node table indexed by 'how many halvings so far' turns an exponential choice tree into a linear-in-depth DP.
hints: At every node you make one of two independent choices, and the choice you make changes what every node in its subtree sees -- so the value seen deep in a subtree depends on how many ancestors already chose the 'halve everything below' option.

Because coin counts are capped, halving the same value more than a dozen or so times always lands on zero -- so you never need to track the exact halving count past that cap, only whether you're at or beyond it.

Do a post-order pass computing, for each node and each possible 'halvings applied by ancestors so far' (0 up to the cap), the best score obtainable in that subtree: either take the node's (halved-by-ancestors) value minus the penalty and recurse at the same halving level, or take half of it and recurse into children at halvings+1 -- take the max of the two at the root with zero ancestor halvings.

edge_inputs.description: star graph, root with three leaf children
all coins zero -- halving is always at least as good as paying k
k is zero -- taking the full amount everywhere is never penalized
k larger than every coin value -- the halve-and-propagate branch should win throughout
deeper chain so a halving decision partway down changes several descendants
max coin value at every node with zero penalty

stress.description: random tree built by attaching each new node to a uniformly random earlier node, random coin values and penalty
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100000))
    edges = []
    for i in range(1, n):
        p = rng.randint(0, i - 1)
        edges.append([p, i])
    coins = [rng.randint(0, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(0, 10000)
    return [edges, coins, k]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def maximumPoints(self, edges, coins, k):
        n = len(coins)
        CAP = 14
        adj = [[] for _ in range(n)]
        for u, v in edges:
            adj[u].append(v)
            adj[v].append(u)
        parent = [-1] * n
        visited = [False] * n
        order = []
        stack = [0]
        visited[0] = True
        while stack:
            node = stack.pop()
            order.append(node)
            for nxt in adj[node]:
                if not visited[nxt]:
                    visited[nxt] = True
                    parent[nxt] = node
                    stack.append(nxt)
        dp = [[0] * (CAP + 1) for _ in range(n)]
        for node in reversed(order):
            for h in range(CAP + 1):
                val = coins[node] >> h
                option_a = val - k
                option_b = val // 2
                for child in adj[node]:
                    if child == parent[node]:
                        continue
                    option_a += dp[child][h]
                    option_b += dp[child][min(h + 1, CAP)]
                dp[node][h] = max(option_a, option_b)
        return dp[0][0]
